Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a set of 10 activities in a supply chain process SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS We need to estimate average times and standard deviations for pairs of activities that eventually follow each other in at least one trace Note The trace is a sequence of these activities in the order they occur We assume that the process is linear and these activities occur in the order listed or at least that order is typical However note that the problem says eventually follow each other so we consider any pair X Y where X comes before Y in the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the process is a sequence we can consider the following pairs in order of occurrence SS OP SS RC up to SS AS OP RC OP QI up to OP AS and so on until DT AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets break down the process and estimate typical times

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Supplier Selection SS to Order Placement OP This might involve negotiations and decision making Lets say average 2 days with standard deviation of 1 day Order Placement OP to Receive Components RC Supplier lead time Average 14 days with standard deviation of 3 days Receive Components RC to Quality Inspection QI Inspection may be scheduled within a day Average 1 day with standard deviation of 0 point 5 day Quality Inspection QI to Component Assembly CA If inspection passes assembly starts Average 0 point 5 day with standard deviation of 0 point 25 day Component Assembly CA to Product Testing PT Assembly time for the product Average 2 days with standard deviation of 1 day Product Testing PT to Packaging PK Testing time Average 1 day with standard deviation of 0 point 5 day Packaging PK to Warehouse Storage WS Packaging time Average 0 point 5 day with standard deviation of 0 point 25 day Warehouse Storage WS to Distribution DT Storage until shipment Average 3 days with standard deviation of 1 day Distribution DT to After Sales Support AS Time from shipment to when after sales support begins maybe after delivery Average 7 days with standard deviation of 2 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now for pairs that are not directly connected we need to add up the times of the steps in between

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: However note that the temporal profile is defined for pairs of activities that eventually follow each other We are to consider all pairs that appear in the event logs We are to provide a representative subset Lets choose a few pairs that are not directly connected to show complexity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Well create a dictionary with the following pairs among others Direct pairs consecutive activities SS OP OP RC RC QI QI CA CA PT PT PK PK WS WS DT DT AS Non direct pairs with one or more steps in between SS RC SS QI SS CA and so on until SS AS Also OP CA OP RC QI CA etc

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to estimate the average and standard deviation for each pair For non direct pairs we can sum the averages and the variances for independent steps but note that in reality the times might be correlated However for the purpose of this exercise we assume independence of the steps Then Total average sum of averages of intermediate steps Total variance sum of variances of intermediate steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets compute for a few pairs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS RC average 172800 plus 1209600 equals 1382400 seconds variance 86400 squared plus 259200 squared stdev about 273200 seconds SS QI Steps SS OP RC QI average 1468800 seconds stdev about 276600 seconds OP CA Steps OP RC QI CA average 1339200 seconds stdev about 263600 seconds SS AS Steps SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS Total 2678400 seconds stdev about 352300 seconds

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Well create a dictionary for a representative subset Lets include All consecutive pairs 9 pairs Then a few non consecutive pairs to illustrate

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: However note that the process is linear so we can compute the cumulative time from the start SS to each activity and then subtract to get the time between two activities Alternatively we can define a function to compute the time between two activities by summing the steps in between

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets define the steps and their statistics steps SS next OP time 172800 var 86400 squared OP next RC time 1209600 var 259200 squared RC next QI time 86400 var 43200 squared QI next CA time 43200 var 21600 squared CA next PT time 172800 var 86400 squared PT next PK time 86400 var 43200 squared PK next WS time 43200 var 21600 squared WS next DT time 259200 var 86400 squared DT next AS time 604800 var 172800 squared

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then for a pair X Y we start from X and follow the steps until we reach Y summing the times and variances We can create a list of activities in order SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS For a pair A B where A comes before B in the order we take the steps from A to B

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets write a function to compute the average and variance for a given pair A B in the order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Find the index of A and B in the order For each step from A to B excluding B add the time and variance of the step from that activity to its next

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Example for SS RC we take steps from SS to OP and OP to RC So we add the time and variance of SS OP and OP RC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In our function we are summing from idx A to idx B minus 1 because the step from activity at index i goes to the next activity which is at index i plus 1 So for WS AS we sum steps for WS and DT So the function is correct

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Now we can compute the time for any pair A B where A comes before B in the order Lets compute the entire set of pairs and create the dictionary

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: However note that the problem says representative subset We can compute all pairs but the dictionary would be 45 pairs We are allowed to choose a subset But to be thorough lets compute all pairs

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Alternatively we can output a subset that includes all consecutive pairs and a few non consecutive pairs that are far apart But since the problem does not specify lets output the entire dictionary

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However 45 pairs is a lot Well write a subset Lets choose 10 pairs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets choose 10 pairs that are a mix of consecutive and non consecutive

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So our 10 pairs are SS OP SS RC SS CA SS PT SS PK SS DT SS AS OP PT RC DT QI AS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now we compute the time for each of these pairs Well use the function we defined

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SS OP time 172800 stdev 86400 SS RC time 1382400 stdev about 273200 SS CA time 1512000 stdev about 277400 SS PT time 1684800 stdev about 290600 SS PK time 1771200 stdev about 293800 SS DT time 2073600 stdev about 307000 SS AS time 2678400 stdev about 352300 OP PT time 1512000 stdev about 277400 RC DT time 691200 stdev about 139980 QI AS time 1209600 stdev about 218100

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We can round to the nearest integer So the dictionary is as above
